title: 张赛：做工厂与送外卖，是我人生的解脱 | 正午故事
title_zh: 张赛：做工厂与送外卖，是我人生的解脱
summary_en: This is the story of Zhang Sai, a lifelong laborer who has spent over twenty years working in factories and as a food delivery worker. He recounts the intense repetitiveness, danger, and physical toll of machine‐based factory labor, contrasted with the relative autonomy of delivering food—even though that work too demands long hours and constant physical effort. Zhang has carried a love of reading and writing through his years of labor, finding in books a refuge for thought and dignity. The COVID‐19 pandemic was a turning point: it brought both fear and greater visibility of the working class’s hardships, and pushed him to write and share more of his and his coworkers’ experiences. His first book about these years, “In Factories One Cannot Dream of Factories: Thus Working Twenty Years”, though not changing his material condition greatly, affirmed his personal agency and preserved his inner life.
summary_zh: 这是张赛的故事，他二十多年在工厂与送外卖中度过。工厂里的工作重复、危险、消耗身体，与外卖工作相比，后者虽然也辛苦、时长且压力大，但至少有些许自由。张赛一直热爱读书与写作，把书籍视为避难所，维持思想与尊严。新冠疫情时期成为他人生的转折，带来恐惧，也让公众更关注打工人的处境，促使他把自己和工友的经历写下来。他新出的一本书《在工厂梦不到工厂：如此工作二十年》，虽然没有显著改善他的物质条件，却提升了他的主体性，保存了他的精神世界。
best_quote_en: “Doing factory work is liberation from delivering food; delivering food is liberation from factory work; factory plus delivery is my life’s liberation.”
best_quote_zh: “做工厂是送外卖的解脱，送外卖是做工厂的解脱，做工厂加上送外卖，是我人生的解脱。”

title: 占有兵：我在广东当保安的日子
title_zh: 占有兵：我在广东当保安的日子
source: 界面新闻 · 正午书架
summary_en: This article recounts the life of Zhan Youbing, who moved to Guangdong in 1995 and worked as a security guard in various hotels and factories. Over decades he documented the daily struggles, small joys, injustices, and resilience of migrant laborers through photography and writing. It describes his living conditions, work hardships, moments of dignity (like interacting with guests or performing small acts of kindness), and also darker episodes such as tragedies among workers, physical & emotional stress, discrimination, and the lack of social welfare benefits for non-local registrants, until he becomes a supervisor of security staff in a factory and later publishes a photo book chronicling 30 years of this life.
summary_zh: 本文讲述了占有兵一个外地打工者在广东从1995 年开始当保安的经历。他先在酒店大堂等岗位，每天八小时站岗、包吃住，工资很低；后来去机场大酒店，环境稍好，也有小小福利与新鲜体验；再后来，他在不同工厂做保安，见证了工人生活的艰辛，比如宿舍拥挤、睡眠不足、情绪压力大、没有户籍待遇等；中间也有温暖和美好，如第一次坐飞机、第一次吃西餐、与同乡相互扶持等。占有兵同时用摄影记录下这些场景，后来出版书籍，用图片与故事展现打工人的生活与尊严。
best_quote_en: I reserve most of my meager earnings; apart from laundry soap and necessary toiletries, I scarcely spend on anything. One time I spent 10 yuan at a game machine—by far the most indulgent thing I ever did.
best_quote_zh: 虽然每月只有450元工资，但我每月会节余500元左右，其中一部分是获得的小费。我对自己比较苛刻，除了必需的洗衣粉和香皂，我几乎不会花钱买任何东西。只记得有一次和同事到蚌岗玩，他们去玩宾果游戏机，我也花了10元玩了一回，那是最奢侈的一次。

title: 宋泽毅：三线建设与一代人的工业记忆
title_zh: 宋泽毅：三线建设与一代人的工业记忆
source: 界面新闻 · 正午视觉
summary_en: This article is about Song Zeyi, a photographer whose family were part of China's Third Front (三线) construction. Through his personal history and fieldwork photographing over 200 factories in many provinces, he explores how the Third Front has shaped industrial geography, regional identity, architectural aesthetics, and fragmented memories. He reflects on the emotional and cultural legacy, the architectural artifacts—many now abandoned or under threat—and the people behind them, stressing the importance of preserving these spaces as carriers of memory rather than letting them vanish into forgetfulness.
summary_zh: 本文讲述摄影师宋泽毅与“三线建设”之间的情感与记忆。他是三线建设者的后来者，从小受到家庭迁徙、工业厂矿环境与文化混合的影响。他开展一个摄影项目，走遍多个省份，对200多家三线厂房进行田野摄影与调研，记录这些厂房的建筑美学、厂区的日常，以及其中人们的情感归属和身份认同。他观察到许多三线建筑正在消失，有些功能废弃，有些被拆毁。他希望通过影像保留那些还在的物理空间，以唤起公众对这段历史与工业文化遗产的关注。
best_quote_en: "Not nostalgia, but memory: one should remember!"

title: Interview with Miner Poet Chen Nianxi: If There Is the Longest Lasting Shout, That Is My Poetry
title_zh: 专访矿工诗人陈年喜:如果有最长久的呐喊,那就是我的诗歌
source: The Paper - Pengpai News · The PaiKe
summary_en: An in-depth interview with Chen Nianxi, a former miner who worked for 16 years and later became a renowned poet, diagnosed with pneumoconiosis in 2020. He discusses his life, the fate of migrant workers, the role of poetry as a lasting shout, and his perspectives on literature and success.
summary_zh: 对矿工诗人陈年喜的深度访谈。他从事矿山爆破工作十六年，2020年确诊尘肺病。访谈探讨了他的生活、打工兄弟的命运、诗歌作为长久呐喊的意义，以及他对文学和成功的看法。
best_quote_en: "If there is the longest lasting shout, that is my poetry and my life-and-death struggle all over the country."
best_quote_zh: "如果有最长久的呐喊,那就是我的诗歌和我不避生死的天南海北的打拼。"

title: 纪念许立志:从“打工文学”到“工人文学”的可能
title_zh: 纪念许立志:从“打工文学”到“工人文学”的可能
summary_en: A deep analysis of Xu Lizhi's poetry and suicide, exploring the potential transition from 'migrant worker literature' to a genuine 'working-class literature' with class consciousness in contemporary China.
summary_zh: 一篇对许立志诗歌及其自杀的深度分析文章，探讨了在当代中国，从“打工文学”向一种具有阶级意识的真正“工人文学”转变的可能性。
best_quote_en: In the darkness, the black eyes we gained should continue to search for light in the darkness; it is indeed difficult to distinguish in daylight which beam of light might be the one that never goes out.
best_quote_zh: “在黑夜中得到的黑色的眼睛，也该在黑夜中继续寻找光明；在白日分辨哪一束光可能是不会熄灭的那一个，确实很难。”

title: Michael Sandel's 'The Tyranny of Merit' reviewed by Spencer Lenfield
title_zh: 斯宾塞·李·伦菲尔德评《The Tyranny of Merit》
summary_en: Spencer Lenfield provides a detailed review of Michael Sandel's 'The Tyranny of Merit,' highlighting how meritocratic systems amplify social inequality, erode the sense of common good, and foster moral arrogance among the successful. Sandel argues that treating success solely as a product of personal effort neglects the role of luck, inheritance, and social circumstances, leading to societal resentment and political polarization.
summary_zh: 斯宾塞·李·伦菲尔德对迈克尔·桑德尔的《The Tyranny of Merit》进行了深入评审，强调精英主义体系如何加剧社会不平等，削弱公共利益意识，并导致成功者的道德傲慢。桑德尔指出，将成功完全归因于个人努力，会忽视运气、家庭背景和社会环境的影响，从而引发社会不满和政治极化。
best_quote_en: Believing that your personal success is entirely due to your own efforts blinds you to the role of luck, family support, and social circumstances, setting a moral trap that diminishes empathy for those less fortunate.
best_quote_zh: 认为个人成功完全源于自身努力，会忽视运气、家庭支持和社会环境的作用，这种心态是一种道德陷阱，使人对处境不利者缺乏同情。

title: Zhong Yongfeng: Neither Social nor Cultural Movements Should Stay in the Study — Yanjing Interview (Wild Lotus Out of the Village)
title_zh: 钟永丰：不管是社会运动还是文化运动，都不能只待在书房丨燕京访谈（野莲出庄）乐评
tags: Wild Lotus Out of the Village
summary_en: This interview (published on Jan 6, 2021) with lyricist Zhong Yongfeng—conducted for Yanjing Review and reported by Zhong Yuyun—explores the conceptual and practical work behind the 2020 album "Wild Lotus Out of the Village" (生祥樂隊). Zhong, a sociologist-turned-lyricist with experience in local administration and social practice, describes a decade-long engagement with food- and plant-centered writing. He explains that the album's 11 songs take rice, wild fruits, vegetables and other food-types as starting points to narrate village life, ecology, labor, gender, time and the effects of globalization (e.g., migrant labor tied to wild-lotus cultivation). Zhong emphasizes that his food-writing is not meant as mere nostalgia or product-promotion; rather, he transforms ethnographic, botanical and social-material research into lyrics that serve musical and auditory needs. The piece also details the creative collaboration with musician Lin Sheng-xiang (lyrics first, then music), instrumentation choices (e.g., suona phrasing inspired by Indonesian/Thai examples to avoid nostalgic timbre), and Zhong's broader view of "re-ruralization"—how younger returnees must connect traditional knowledge with urban skills. Overall, the interview frames the album as both literary-musical work and situated social practice, stressing fieldwork, dialogue, and entering changing scenes rather than staying confined to the study.
summary_zh: 这篇发表于 2021-01-06 的访谈（撰稿：钟昱赟）采访了作词人钟永丰，围绕生祥乐队 2020 年专辑《野莲出庄》的构思与创作展开。钟永丰作为社会学出身、长期参与地方工作与社会实践的词作者，讲述了他用近十年时间阅读植物、食物与民族志相关材料，尝试把“食物”作为主体来写出村落的“本我”状态。专辑以米食、野果、野菜等食物线索勾勒农村日常与生态、劳工关系、性别与时间等社会议题（例如野莲与外籍劳工、全球化的关联）。他强调歌词必须为听觉服务，写食物不能沦为单纯的地方产品宣传；通过与林生祥的合作与大量田野式的阅读、访谈与对话，把社会学、人类学的视角转换为可听的民谣文本。文章同时讨论了方言的运用、器乐（唢呐）的表现选择、专辑的跨文化混杂元素，以及“再乡土化”与回乡工作者如何接续传统与当代技术的命题。
best_quote_en: "Writing about food easily becomes mere promotion of local products. While food writing can of course have social effects, that was not my initial creative motivation—I must start from literary and musical perspectives and find my position within my own creative trajectory."
best_quote_zh: “写食物，很容易变成宣传地方产品。食物书写，当然有可能产生后续的社会效应，但那不是我创作之初的动力来源和思考重点。我还是得从文学、音乐的角度出发，以及在个人的创作历程中找到定位。”

title: Documentary Critique: Shen Enzhen on 'I Love You, Shamate' — Finding Joy Amid Hardship
title_zh: 纪录片批评 | 沈恩真：《杀马特我爱你》：苦中作乐是生活常态
summary_en: Shen Enzhen's critique reads Li Yifan’s 2019 documentary I Love You, Shamate as a layered portrait of a marginalized youth subculture—the Shamate—framing them not merely as an online joke but as largely post-90s migrant workers whose extravagant style masks deep material and emotional precarity. The review summarizes the film’s provenance (shot over years, screened Dec 2019 at the Guangdong Times Art Museum), Li’s fieldwork (78 Shamate interviewees, 11 conducted online, and ~915 collected clips of factory life), and narrative strategy (interweaving mobile-life footage, interviews, and factory rhythms). Shen highlights key thematic moves: identity formation through exaggerated fashion, the search for belonging amid loneliness and labor exploitation, the gap between popular ridicule and lived vulnerability, and how the film reclaims the Shamate as a social-historical phenomenon rather than mere tasteless spectacle. The critique also notes aesthetic choices—sound design that echoes factory machines, the use of English captions—and closes by arguing the film provides a corrective empathy: while Shamate may fade from public view, the social conditions that produced them persist.
summary_zh: 沈恩真的影评把李一凡 2019 年影片《杀马特我爱你》解读为对被边缘化青年亚文化的多层次呈现：所谓“杀马特”并非单纯的网络笑点，而大多为90后农民工，他们用夸张的装束掩盖脆弱的物质与情感处境。影评交代了影片来龙去脉（拍摄多年、2019年12月在广东时代美术馆放映）、田野工作细节（采访78名杀马特，其中11人以网络方式；收集约915段工厂及生活录像）与叙事手法（生活录像与访谈交织、以流水线声作为叙事线索）。文章强调的主题包括：通过发型与服饰建构身份、在孤独与劳动压榨下寻求归属、公众嘲讽与个体脆弱之间的反差，以及影片如何试图把杀马特还原为一种社会-历史现象而非低俗趣味。影评还点出若干美学选择（如将工厂流水声融入配乐、使用英文字幕带来的语境冲突），并总结认为：即便公众舆论使杀马特式的表象消退，其背后的社会条件仍然存在。
best_quote_en: "I didn't set out to film the history of Shamate; I filmed a part of the history of post-90s migrant workers."
best_quote_zh: “我拍的不是杀马特史，我拍的是90后农民工历史的一部分。”

title: Interview: Huang Deng — 'My Second-Tier Students': Four Years of College, They Never Stopped Involution
title_zh: 专访｜黄灯《我的二本学生》：大学四年，他们从未停止内卷
summary_en: This is an in-depth interview with author and teacher Huang Deng about her book "My Second-Tier Students" (published by People's Literature Publishing House in August 2020). Drawing on a roster of over 4,500 former students, Huang selected more than 100 cases to trace the educational and life trajectories of students who attend China’s abundant second-tier (二本) universities. The piece situates these students within the broader context of higher-education expansion: by mid-year there were 3,005 higher-education institutions nationwide and only a small fraction belong to elite '211/985' schools; the majority of enrolled students study at second/third-tier or vocational colleges. Huang argues that, contrary to the myth that “going to university is liberation,” many second-tier students experience continuous pressure—exam prep, graduate-school competition, job hunting, and housing anxiety—so that their four university years are marked by persistent "involution." Through comparative vignettes (classes of 2006 vs. 2015) and concrete portraits (e.g., Yang Shengxuan, Xiushan), the interview links changes in student aspirations and outcomes to family resources, regional economy, and labor market shifts. Huang also reflects on the limits and responsibilities of nonfiction recording, the ethical choices in naming/masking, and the surprising empathy her book elicited across class and school lines.
summary_zh: 本文为澎湃新闻对作者兼教师黄灯的专访，围绕其作品《我的二本学生》（人民文学出版社，2020年8月出版）展开。黄灯以其花名册上4500余名学生为基础，选取100多名个案，记录了大量就读中国二本院校学生的教育与人生轨迹。文章把个体经历放在高等教育大众化的大背景中考察：截至当年6月，全国高等学府达3005所，本科院校1258所，‘211/985’只占少数，而大多数在校生在二本、三本及专科院校就读。黄灯指出，大学并非对多数学生而言的“解放”，二本学生在四年里仍然被考研、就业、买房等压力逼着“内卷”着度过。通过对比2006级与2015级的课堂与个案（如杨胜轩、秀珊），专访展示了家庭资源、地区差异与劳动力市场如何影响学生的命运。同时，黄灯讨论了纪实写作的局限与伦理、将教室观察转化为文本的方法，以及书稿发布后跨阶层读者反响带来的意外影响。
best_quote_en: “They never stopped 'involution' during four years of college.”
best_quote_zh: “大学四年，他们从未停止内卷。”

title: Wang Bing’s "Youth": Portrait of Young People in a Garment Town
title_zh: 王兵《青春》：一座制衣小镇里的青年群像
summary_en: This article is an interview with filmmaker Wang Bing about his documentary series "Youth", focusing on the lives of young garment workers in Zhili, a small manufacturing town roughly 150 km from Shanghai. From 2014 to 2019, Wang and his team accumulated over 2,600 hours of footage to depict daily rhythms of work, hopes for future, friendships, love, and the tension between aspiration and labor. The textile industry in Zhili is decentralized, run by many small units and cottage operations, giving young migrants both autonomy and precarity. Wang highlights how youth cope with monotony via small pleasures, how the social fabric links them with home villages, and how they negotiate identity under economic strains. Aesthetic and narrative choices—episodic structure, long takes, observing rather than imposing dramatic arcs—lend the film a poetic realism.
summary_zh: 本文为导演王兵关于《青春》系列纪录片的访谈，纪录了制衣小镇织里年轻务工者的生活状态。影片拍摄时间从2014年延续到2019年，累积约 2600 小时素材，聚焦他们日复一日的缝纫工作、友情、爱情与对未来的期望。织里制衣业高度分散，由成千上万的小作坊和家庭作坊组成，这种生态既带来自主性，也伴随着不稳定性与劳动剥削。王兵强调青年人在单调工作中通过嬉闹、零食、音乐、谈恋爱来缓解，以及他们与家乡村落的情感牵连。他在叙事与美学上的选择，如篇章式结构、长镜头、淡化戏剧冲突，体现一种诗意写实主义。
best_quote_en: "From 2014 to 2019, I accumulated over 2,600 hours of footage—the lives in their dorms, their moments of rest, their small rebellions—because it's in the in-between that the real youth shows itself."
best_quote_zh: “从2014年到2019年，我积累了约2600小时的素材——宿舍里的生活、休息间的瞬间、小小的反抗——因为青年真正的面貌是在这些缝隙里显露出来的。”

title: Dialogue between Michael Sandel and Xiang Biao: How Can We Escape Meritocracy?
title_zh: 项飙对谈迈克尔·桑德尔：我们如何摆脱“优绩主义”？
summary_en: The dialogue between political philosopher Michael Sandel and anthropologist Xiang Biao explores the pitfalls of meritocracy. Sandel argues that meritocracy, while seemingly just, creates moral arrogance among winners and shame among losers, deepening inequality and eroding public life. Xiang contrasts ancient Chinese meritocracy, rooted in virtue, with today’s universalized and competitive version. They discuss how education has become a sorting machine, fueling social anxiety in both the U.S. and China. To move beyond meritocracy’s tyranny, Sandel calls for broader definitions of merit, recognition of public virtues, and rebuilding shared public spaces. Xiang emphasizes 'finding the nearby'—returning to individual life contexts to resist homogenized ideals of success.
summary_zh: 哈佛大学政治哲学家迈克尔·桑德尔与社会人类学家项飙探讨了优绩主义的陷阱。桑德尔指出，优绩主义表面公平，实则让胜利者产生道德傲慢，失败者陷入羞辱和怨恨，从而加剧不平等，侵蚀公共生活。项飙则对比了中国古代基于德行的优绩主义与当今普遍化、竞争化的版本，指出其在教育和社会分层中带来的焦虑。对话中，桑德尔强调要打破单一的经济价值衡量，重视公共美德与共同体贡献，并重建公共空间与交流渠道；项飙则强调“找回附近”，从个体生活出发抵抗同质化的成功定义，寻找多样的生活方式与社会可能。
best_quote_en: Meritocracy’s cruelest feature is that it makes winners forget luck and society’s help, and makes losers believe they fully deserve their fate.
best_quote_zh: 优绩主义最残酷的一面在于，它让胜利者忘记运气与社会的帮助，让失败者相信失败完全是自己应得的。

title: 回归故里——从法国旧故事重返工人文化
title_zh: 回归故里——从法国旧故事重返工人文化
summary_en: This article reviews Didier Eribon's autobiographical work 'Returning to Reims', discussing the author's cultural alienation upon returning to his working-class hometown in France. Zhang Jieying analyzes Eribon's reflections on social class, the French education system, and the reproduction of elites. The review explores how working-class youths navigate education, social mobility, and identity, highlighting cross-cultural parallels, including China.
summary_zh: 本文书评了迪迪埃·埃里蓬自传性著作《回归故里》，讲述作者重返法国工人阶级家乡时的文化疏离感。张劼颖从社会阶级、法国教育制度及精英再生产等角度进行分析，探讨工人子弟如何在教育、阶级流动和身份认同中挣扎，同时指出与中国的跨文化相似现象。
best_quote_en: Merely escaping one's social origin does not free one from the cultural imprint of class; the discomfort upon returning home reveals the deep structures that shape lives.
best_quote_zh: 仅仅逃离自己的社会出身并不能摆脱阶级的文化烙印；回到家乡的不适感揭示了深层塑造生活的社会结构。

title: 林小英谈县中的孩子
summary_en: In this interview, Lin Xiaoying discusses her book 'Children in County Schools,' exploring the challenges faced by students and teachers in rural education, the impact of educational inequality, and the search for meaningful learning experiences.
summary_zh: 在这次访谈中，林小英讨论了她的著作《县中的孩子》，探讨了农村教育中学生和教师面临的挑战、教育不平等的影响以及寻找有意义的学习体验的问题。
best_quote_en: 真正的意义感是通过什么样的活动而生发的？就是多去做做那些跟眼下谋生不太相关的事，这恰恰是产生意义的重要领域。
best_quote_zh: 真正的意义感是通过什么样的活动而生发的？就是多去做做那些跟眼下谋生不太相关的事，这恰恰是产生意义的重要领域。

title: “读书的料”及其文化生产
summary_en: The article summarizes a scholarly discussion of Cheng Meng's book 'The Material for Study' and explores how rural children who enter elite universities navigate social and educational landscapes. The focus is on their cultural production, moral obligations to family, and the structural challenges they face while seeking upward mobility.
summary_zh: 本文总结了程猛老师《“读书的料”及其文化生产》一书的学术分享，探讨了进入精英大学的农家子弟如何穿行于社会与教育场域中，关注他们的文化生产、对家庭的道德义务，以及在阶层流动过程中面临的制度性挑战。
best_quote_en: Rural students navigating education are not mere recipients of cultural capital; their journey is shaped by moral obligations, personal agency, and social structures.
best_quote_zh: 农家子弟在教育过程中并非文化资本的被动接受者；他们的成长历程由家庭道德义务、个体能动性和社会结构共同塑造。

title: 《美国工厂》：全球化时代的冲突与反思
title_zh: 《美国工厂》：全球化时代的冲突与反思
summary_en: This review deeply examines the documentary 'American Factory', highlighting the challenges of labor relations, cultural clashes between American and Chinese workers, and the broader impact of globalization on industrial workforces.
summary_zh: 这篇影评深入分析了纪录片《美国工厂》，聚焦于劳工关系的挑战、中美工人之间的文化冲突，以及全球化对工业劳动者的广泛影响。
best_quote_en: 'American Factory' unveils the struggles of workers and the cultural tensions that emerge when globalization reshapes traditional labor landscapes, revealing both human and systemic challenges.
best_quote_zh: 《美国工厂》揭示了工人的困境，以及全球化重塑传统劳动格局时产生的文化紧张，呈现了人性与制度上的多重挑战。

title: 〖专访〗学者金雯：年轻人对爱的幻灭，已成为他们日常生活政治的一部分
title_zh: 〖专访〗学者金雯：年轻人对爱的幻灭，已成为他们日常生活政治的一部分
source: 界面新闻 / 界面文化
summary_en: In this interview, scholar Jin Wen explores the ‘disillusionment of love’ among young people, tracing it through the history of sentimental literature from the 18th century and relating it to contemporary emotional anxieties. She argues that what was once a space for romantic adventure has become a cautious terrain shaped by economic, social, and emotional pressures.
summary_zh: 在这次访谈中，学者金雯探讨了当代年轻人对爱情的幻灭感，从18世纪的感伤小说史入手，将其与当下情感焦虑连结起来。她认为，本应是浪漫冒险的情感空间，如今已被经济、社会与情绪压力所塑造，成为一种谨慎的情感地带。
best_quote_en: Many young people claim they no longer allow emotions to move them—this doesn’t show strength, but a surrender.
best_quote_zh: 很多年轻人声称自己不再让情感打动自己——这并不是一种坚强，而是一种投降。
